faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Oracle intelligence

questions.question: How does Oracle's AI analysis work?
questions.answer: Oracle uses advanced machine learning models trained on millions of security events. It analyzes patterns, anomalies, and correlations in your data to identify threats and predict future security events. The AI continuously learns from new data to improve accuracy.

questions.question: What data sources does Oracle analyze?
questions.answer: Oracle can analyze multiple data sources including system logs, network traffic, application metrics, security events, threat intelligence feeds, and data from the Mycelium Network. You can customize which sources to include based on your infrastructure and security needs.

questions.question: How accurate is Oracle's threat prediction?
questions.answer: Oracle achieves 99.7% detection accuracy through its advanced AI models and continuous learning. The predictive capabilities have been validated across thousands of deployments and continuously improve with each analysis.

questions.question: Can Oracle integrate with our existing security tools?
questions.answer: Yes, Oracle provides extensive integration capabilities including REST APIs, webhooks, and native connectors for popular SIEM, SOAR, and monitoring platforms. Our team can also help with custom integrations for your specific tools.

questions.question: How quickly does Oracle detect threats?
questions.answer: Oracle provides real-time threat detection with an average response time of 250ms. Critical threats are identified and alerted instantly, while comprehensive analysis completes within seconds depending on data volume and complexity.

questions.question: Is Oracle suitable for compliance requirements?
questions.answer: Absolutely. Oracle helps with compliance monitoring, automated reporting, and audit trail maintenance. It supports major compliance frameworks including GDPR, SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, and industry-specific regulations.
